entries.title: You use it every day but how does it work? A refrigerator
entries.hook: You use it every day but how does it work?
explanations: Liquid refrigerant evaporates inside absorbing heat. A compressor squeezes it back to liquid outside releasing the heat.
Spinning blades at an angle push air downward for wind chill. Reversing circulates warm trapped air back down.
Pedals turn a chainring that drives the rear wheel through a chain. Gears change the ratio for speed or climbing.
Electricity excites mercury vapor emitting UV light. UV hits a phosphor coating inside the tube which glows visible white.

entries.title: Ever wondered how this works? A zipper
explanations: A zipper works using interlocking teeth. The slider forces teeth together locking hook into hollow. Pulling down splits them apart.
A microwave shoots waves at 2.4 gigahertz that excite water molecules in food making them vibrate and create friction heat.
Liquid refrigerant evaporates inside absorbing heat. A compressor squeezes it back to liquid outside releasing the heat.
A small magnetized needle aligns with Earth's magnetic field always pointing north. The housing lets you orient yourself.

A tiny radioactive source ionizes air between electrodes. Smoke particles disrupt the current triggering the alarm.

entries.title: Here's how it actually works: A toilet
explanations: Flushing opens a valve. Water rushes from tank to bowl creating a siphon that pulls everything out. The tank then refills.
A sliding mechanism pushes metal ribs outward stretching fabric into a dome. The curved shape deflects rain and wind.
Pedals turn a chainring that drives the rear wheel through a chain. Gears change the ratio for speed or climbing.
Electric current passes through nichrome wires which resist electricity and glow red hot. The heat toasts the bread.

The curved shape deflects rain and wind. Graphite flakes stick to paper through friction. The clay binder adjusts hardness. Eraser removes flakes using abrasion.

explanations: Light enters through the lens. The shutter opens briefly to let light hit the sensor converting photons into a digital image.
Pedals turn a chainring that drives the rear wheel through a chain. Gears change the ratio for speed or climbing.
Electricity excites mercury vapor emitting UV light. UV hits a phosphor coating inside the tube which glows visible white.
Spring-loaded pins in a cylinder are pushed up by the key's ridges. When all pins align at the shear line the shackle releases.

A sliding mechanism pushes metal ribs outward stretching fabric into a dome. The curved shape deflects rain and wind.

explanations: A zipper works using interlocking teeth. The slider forces teeth together locking hook into hollow. Pulling down splits them apart.
Two different metals separated by electrolyte. When connected chemical reaction sends electrons through the wire creating electricity.
A motor turns a chain loop that pulls steps in a circle. Wheels on two tracks flatten at ends so you can step on and off.
Spring-loaded pins in a cylinder are pushed up by the key's ridges. When all pins align at the shear line the shackle releases.

entries.title: You use it every day but how does it work? A refrigerator
entries.hook: You use it every day but how does it work?
explanations: Liquid refrigerant evaporates inside absorbing heat. A compressor squeezes it back to liquid outside releasing the heat.
A motor spins a fan that sucks air in creating low pressure. Outside air pushes in carrying dirt. Filters trap particles.
A sliding mechanism pushes metal ribs outward stretching fabric into a dome. The curved shape deflects rain and wind.
Electric current passes through nichrome wires which resist electricity and glow red hot. The heat toasts the bread.

The shutter opens briefly to let light hit the sensor converting photons into a digital image. A tiny radioactive source ionizes air between electrodes. Smoke particles disrupt the current triggering the alarm. A small magnetized needle aligns with Earth's magnetic field always pointing north. The housing lets you orient yourself.

entries.title: Ever wondered how this works? An escalator
explanations: A motor turns a chain loop that pulls steps in a circle. Wheels on two tracks flatten at ends so you can step on and off.
A sliding mechanism pushes metal ribs outward stretching fabric into a dome. The curved shape deflects rain and wind.
An electromagnet pushes and pulls against a permanent magnet moving a cone back and forth. The cone vibrates air creating sound.
Spring-loaded pins in a cylinder are pushed up by the key's ridges. When all pins align at the shear line the shackle releases.

explanations: Liquid refrigerant evaporates inside absorbing heat. A compressor squeezes it back to liquid outside releasing the heat.
Spinning blades at an angle push air downward for wind chill. Reversing circulates warm trapped air back down.
A tiny radioactive source ionizes air between electrodes. Smoke particles disrupt the current triggering the alarm.
A sliding mechanism pushes metal ribs outward stretching fabric into a dome. The curved shape deflects rain and wind.

entries.title: Here's how it actually works: An escalator
explanations: A motor turns a chain loop that pulls steps in a circle. Wheels on two tracks flatten at ends so you can step on and off.
Spinning blades at an angle push air downward for wind chill. Reversing circulates warm trapped air back down.
A sliding mechanism pushes metal ribs outward stretching fabric into a dome. The curved shape deflects rain and wind.
Graphite flakes stick to paper through friction. The clay binder adjusts hardness. Eraser removes flakes using abrasion.

explanations: A microwave shoots waves at 2.4 gigahertz that excite water molecules in food making them vibrate and create friction heat.

explanations: A microwave shoots waves at 2.4 gigahertz that excite water molecules in food making them vibrate and create friction heat.
Two different metals separated by electrolyte. When connected chemical reaction sends electrons through the wire creating electricity.
A small magnetized needle aligns with Earth's magnetic field always pointing north. The housing lets you orient yourself.
Electric current passes through nichrome wires which resist electricity and glow red hot. The heat toasts the bread.

entries.title: Here's how it actually works: A zipper
explanations: A zipper works using interlocking teeth. The slider forces teeth together locking hook into hollow. Pulling down splits them apart.
Two different metals separated by electrolyte. When connected chemical reaction sends electrons through the wire creating electricity.
An electromagnet pushes and pulls against a permanent magnet moving a cone back and forth. The cone vibrates air creating sound.
Spring-loaded pins in a cylinder are pushed up by the key's ridges. When all pins align at the shear line the shackle releases.

entries.title: Let me explain how this works: An escalator
entries.hook: Let me explain how this works:
explanations: A motor turns a chain loop that pulls steps in a circle. Wheels on two tracks flatten at ends so you can step on and off.
Pedals turn a chainring that drives the rear wheel through a chain. Gears change the ratio for speed or climbing.
Electricity excites mercury vapor emitting UV light. UV hits a phosphor coating inside the tube which glows visible white.
Graphite flakes stick to paper through friction. The clay binder adjusts hardness. Eraser removes flakes using abrasion.

entries.title: Here's how it actually works: A refrigerator
explanations: Liquid refrigerant evaporates inside absorbing heat. A compressor squeezes it back to liquid outside releasing the heat.
Flushing opens a valve. Water rushes from tank to bowl creating a siphon that pulls everything out. The tank then refills.
Spinning blades at an angle push air downward for wind chill. Reversing circulates warm trapped air back down.
A motor spins a fan that sucks air in creating low pressure. Outside air pushes in carrying dirt. Filters trap particles.

entries.title: You use it every day but how does it work? A smoke detector
entries.hook: You use it every day but how does it work?
explanations: A tiny radioactive source ionizes air between electrodes. Smoke particles disrupt the current triggering the alarm.
A sliding mechanism pushes metal ribs outward stretching fabric into a dome. The curved shape deflects rain and wind.
A small magnetized needle aligns with Earth's magnetic field always pointing north. The housing lets you orient yourself.
An electromagnet pushes and pulls against a permanent magnet moving a cone back and forth. The cone vibrates air creating sound.

entries.title: Let me explain how this works: A zipper
entries.hook: Let me explain how this works:
explanations: A zipper works using interlocking teeth. The slider forces teeth together locking hook into hollow. Pulling down splits them apart.
Spring-loaded pins block the cylinder. The correct key pushes pins to the right height so the cylinder can rotate and unlock.
Spring-loaded pins in a cylinder are pushed up by the key's ridges. When all pins align at the shear line the shackle releases.
A needle pushes thread through fabric. A shuttle below catches the thread with a second bobbin thread locking the stitch.

entries.title: Let me explain how this works: A microwave
entries.hook: Let me explain how this works:
explanations: A microwave shoots waves at 2.4 gigahertz that excite water molecules in food making them vibrate and create friction heat.
A motor spins a fan that sucks air in creating low pressure. Outside air pushes in carrying dirt. Filters trap particles.
Pedals turn a chainring that drives the rear wheel through a chain. Gears change the ratio for speed or climbing.
Electric current passes through nichrome wires which resist electricity and glow red hot. The heat toasts the bread.

A tiny radioactive source ionizes air between electrodes. Smoke particles disrupt the current triggering the alarm.
Electric current passes through nichrome wires which resist electricity and glow red hot. The heat toasts the bread.
A needle pushes thread through fabric. A shuttle below catches the thread with a second bobbin thread locking the stitch.

entries.title: You use it every day but how does it work? A smoke detector
entries.hook: You use it every day but how does it work?
explanations: A tiny radioactive source ionizes air between electrodes. Smoke particles disrupt the current triggering the alarm.
A sliding mechanism pushes metal ribs outward stretching fabric into a dome. The curved shape deflects rain and wind.
A small magnetized needle aligns with Earth's magnetic field always pointing north. The housing lets you orient yourself.
Graphite flakes stick to paper through friction. The clay binder adjusts hardness. Eraser removes flakes using abrasion.

entries.title: Ever wondered how this works? A refrigerator
explanations: Liquid refrigerant evaporates inside absorbing heat. A compressor squeezes it back to liquid outside releasing the heat.
A motor turns a chain loop that pulls steps in a circle. Wheels on two tracks flatten at ends so you can step on and off.
Graphite flakes stick to paper through friction. The clay binder adjusts hardness. Eraser removes flakes using abrasion.
Spring-loaded pins in a cylinder are pushed up by the key's ridges. When all pins align at the shear line the shackle releases.

entries.title: You use it every day but how does it work? A zipper
entries.hook: You use it every day but how does it work?
explanations: A zipper works using interlocking teeth. The slider forces teeth together locking hook into hollow. Pulling down splits them apart.
A motor turns a chain loop that pulls steps in a circle. Wheels on two tracks flatten at ends so you can step on and off.
Pedals turn a chainring that drives the rear wheel through a chain. Gears change the ratio for speed or climbing.
Electric current passes through nichrome wires which resist electricity and glow red hot. The heat toasts the bread.

Spinning blades at an angle push air downward for wind chill. Reversing circulates warm trapped air back down.
Graphite flakes stick to paper through friction. The clay binder adjusts hardness. Eraser removes flakes using abrasion.
An electromagnet pushes and pulls against a permanent magnet moving a cone back and forth. The cone vibrates air creating sound.

entries.title: You use it every day but how does it work? A zipper
entries.hook: You use it every day but how does it work?
explanations: A zipper works using interlocking teeth. The slider forces teeth together locking hook into hollow. Pulling down splits them apart.
Liquid refrigerant evaporates inside absorbing heat. A compressor squeezes it back to liquid outside releasing the heat.
A tiny radioactive source ionizes air between electrodes. Smoke particles disrupt the current triggering the alarm.
A needle pushes thread through fabric. A shuttle below catches the thread with a second bobbin thread locking the stitch.

entries.title: Let me explain how this works: A battery
entries.hook: Let me explain how this works:
explanations: Two different metals separated by electrolyte. When connected chemical reaction sends electrons through the wire creating electricity.
A motor turns a chain loop that pulls steps in a circle. Wheels on two tracks flatten at ends so you can step on and off.
A tiny radioactive source ionizes air between electrodes. Smoke particles disrupt the current triggering the alarm.
Graphite flakes stick to paper through friction. The clay binder adjusts hardness. Eraser removes flakes using abrasion.

entries.title: You use it every day but how does it work? A microwave
entries.hook: You use it every day but how does it work?
explanations: A microwave shoots waves at 2.4 gigahertz that excite water molecules in food making them vibrate and create friction heat.
Liquid refrigerant evaporates inside absorbing heat. A compressor squeezes it back to liquid outside releasing the heat.
Flushing opens a valve. Water rushes from tank to bowl creating a siphon that pulls everything out. The tank then refills.
Graphite flakes stick to paper through friction. The clay binder adjusts hardness. Eraser removes flakes using abrasion.

entries.title: You use it every day but how does it work? An escalator
entries.hook: You use it every day but how does it work?
explanations: A motor turns a chain loop that pulls steps in a circle. Wheels on two tracks flatten at ends so you can step on and off.
Pedals turn a chainring that drives the rear wheel through a chain. Gears change the ratio for speed or climbing.
Spring-loaded pins in a cylinder are pushed up by the key's ridges. When all pins align at the shear line the shackle releases.
A needle pushes thread through fabric. A shuttle below catches the thread with a second bobbin thread locking the stitch.

explanations: A microwave shoots waves at 2.4 gigahertz that excite water molecules in food making them vibrate and create friction heat.
Two different metals separated by electrolyte. When connected chemical reaction sends electrons through the wire creating electricity.
A tiny radioactive source ionizes air between electrodes. Smoke particles disrupt the current triggering the alarm.
Pedals turn a chainring that drives the rear wheel through a chain. Gears change the ratio for speed or climbing.

entries.title: Let me explain how this works: A zipper
entries.hook: Let me explain how this works:
explanations: A zipper works using interlocking teeth. The slider forces teeth together locking hook into hollow. Pulling down splits them apart.
Flushing opens a valve. Water rushes from tank to bowl creating a siphon that pulls everything out. The tank then refills.
Electric current passes through nichrome wires which resist electricity and glow red hot. The heat toasts the bread.
Graphite flakes stick to paper through friction. The clay binder adjusts hardness. Eraser removes flakes using abrasion.

Liquid refrigerant evaporates inside absorbing heat. A compressor squeezes it back to liquid outside releasing the heat.

A motor spins a fan that sucks air in creating low pressure. Outside air pushes in carrying dirt. Filters trap particles.
A small magnetized needle aligns with Earth's magnetic field always pointing north. The housing lets you orient yourself.
Spring-loaded pins in a cylinder are pushed up by the key's ridges. When all pins align at the shear line the shackle releases.

explanations: A zipper works using interlocking teeth. The slider forces teeth together locking hook into hollow. Pulling down splits them apart.
Two different metals separated by electrolyte. When connected chemical reaction sends electrons through the wire creating electricity.

Spinning blades at an angle push air downward for wind chill. Reversing circulates warm trapped air back down.

entries.title: You use it every day but how does it work? A lock and key
entries.hook: You use it every day but how does it work?
explanations: Spring-loaded pins block the cylinder. The correct key pushes pins to the right height so the cylinder can rotate and unlock.

A small magnetized needle aligns with Earth's magnetic field always pointing north. The housing lets you orient yourself.
Electric current passes through nichrome wires which resist electricity and glow red hot. The heat toasts the bread.
Spring-loaded pins in a cylinder are pushed up by the key's ridges. When all pins align at the shear line the shackle releases.

entries.title: You use it every day but how does it work? An escalator
entries.hook: You use it every day but how does it work?
explanations: A motor turns a chain loop that pulls steps in a circle. Wheels on two tracks flatten at ends so you can step on and off.
A tiny radioactive source ionizes air between electrodes. Smoke particles disrupt the current triggering the alarm.
A motor spins a fan that sucks air in creating low pressure. Outside air pushes in carrying dirt. Filters trap particles.
Pedals turn a chainring that drives the rear wheel through a chain. Gears change the ratio for speed or climbing.

entries.title: Let me explain how this works: A microwave
entries.hook: Let me explain how this works:
explanations: A microwave shoots waves at 2.4 gigahertz that excite water molecules in food making them vibrate and create friction heat.
Two different metals separated by electrolyte. When connected chemical reaction sends electrons through the wire creating electricity.
Light enters through the lens. The shutter opens briefly to let light hit the sensor converting photons into a digital image.
A motor turns a chain loop that pulls steps in a circle. Wheels on two tracks flatten at ends so you can step on and off.

entries.title: Here's how it actually works: A lock and key
explanations: Spring-loaded pins block the cylinder. The correct key pushes pins to the right height so the cylinder can rotate and unlock.
A tiny radioactive source ionizes air between electrodes. Smoke particles disrupt the current triggering the alarm.
A sliding mechanism pushes metal ribs outward stretching fabric into a dome. The curved shape deflects rain and wind.
Spring-loaded pins in a cylinder are pushed up by the key's ridges. When all pins align at the shear line the shackle releases.

entries.title: Let me explain how this works: A refrigerator
entries.hook: Let me explain how this works:
explanations: Liquid refrigerant evaporates inside absorbing heat. A compressor squeezes it back to liquid outside releasing the heat.
Flushing opens a valve. Water rushes from tank to bowl creating a siphon that pulls everything out. The tank then refills.
A small magnetized needle aligns with Earth's magnetic field always pointing north. The housing lets you orient yourself.
Electric current passes through nichrome wires which resist electricity and glow red hot. The heat toasts the bread.

A small magnetized needle aligns with Earth's magnetic field always pointing north. The housing lets you orient yourself.

entries.title: Ever wondered how this works? A lock and key
explanations: Spring-loaded pins block the cylinder. The correct key pushes pins to the right height so the cylinder can rotate and unlock.
Light enters through the lens. The shutter opens briefly to let light hit the sensor converting photons into a digital image.
A motor turns a chain loop that pulls steps in a circle. Wheels on two tracks flatten at ends so you can step on and off.
Electric current passes through nichrome wires which resist electricity and glow red hot. The heat toasts the bread.

entries.title: Let me explain how this works: A toilet
entries.hook: Let me explain how this works:
explanations: Flushing opens a valve. Water rushes from tank to bowl creating a siphon that pulls everything out. The tank then refills.
A tiny radioactive source ionizes air between electrodes. Smoke particles disrupt the current triggering the alarm.
A small magnetized needle aligns with Earth's magnetic field always pointing north. The housing lets you orient yourself.
Spring-loaded pins in a cylinder are pushed up by the key's ridges. When all pins align at the shear line the shackle releases.

entries.hook: You use it every day but how does it work?
explanations: Liquid refrigerant evaporates inside absorbing heat. A compressor squeezes it back to liquid outside releasing the heat.
Flushing opens a valve. Water rushes from tank to bowl creating a siphon that pulls everything out. The tank then refills.
Electricity excites mercury vapor emitting UV light. UV hits a phosphor coating inside the tube which glows visible white.
A needle pushes thread through fabric. A shuttle below catches the thread with a second bobbin thread locking the stitch.
